channel.title: Statistics Notes – Mathematical Statistics 1
channel.description: Recent content in Mathematical Statistics 1 on Statistics Notes

item.title: Sufficiency
item.description:
This $\sigma(z) = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-z}}$ is inline.

$$F(\omega) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f(t) e^{-j\omega t} \, dt$$$$ \begin{aligned} \nabla \cdot \mathbf{E} &= \frac{\rho}{\varepsilon_0} \\ \nabla \cdot \mathbf{B} &= 0 \\ \nabla \times \mathbf{E} &= -\frac{\partial \mathbf{B}}{\partial t} \\ \nabla \times \mathbf{B} &= \mu_0 \left( \mathbf{J} + \varepsilon_0 \frac{\partial \mathbf{E}}{\partial t} \right) \end{aligned} $$
graph TD;
    A-->B;
    A-->C;
    B-->D;
    C-->D;

item.title: Completeness
item.description:
This $\sigma(z) = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-z}}$ is inline.

$$F(\omega) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f(t) e^{-j\omega t} \, dt$$$$ \begin{aligned} \nabla \cdot \mathbf{E} &= \frac{\rho}{\varepsilon_0} \\ \nabla \cdot \mathbf{B} &= 0 \\ \nabla \times \mathbf{E} &= -\frac{\partial \mathbf{B}}{\partial t} \\ \nabla \times \mathbf{B} &= \mu_0 \left( \mathbf{J} + \varepsilon_0 \frac{\partial \mathbf{E}}{\partial t} \right) \end{aligned} $$
graph TD;
    A-->B;
    A-->C;
    B-->D;
    C-->D;
